text: A global memory chip shortage has driven smartphone component costs up thirty percent, IDC reports.
CNN says the average selling price will hit a record five hundred twenty-three dollars this year.
Bloomberg blames the crisis on data center builders hoarding DRAM chips, leaving little for consumer electronics.
IDC also forecasts shipments will plunge twelve point nine percent, the sharpest annual decline ever recorded.
CNBC notes the shortage could permanently reshape the industry, favoring Apple and Samsung.
The AI boom has created a zero-sum game for memory chips, where data centers win and consumers pay the price.
This digital divide between corporations and individuals is widening faster than ever.
